front: Sculpture: bronze relief on Boston Common depicting a colonel on horseback leading black soldiers with an angel of Victory overhead
Sculpture: inscribed with the Latin motto of the Society of Cincinnati: 'He forsook all to preserve the public weal'
Sculpture: created at Henry Hobson Richardson's suggestion; inspired by Meissonier's Campaign of France 1814
Sculpture: African-American model Hettie Anderson posed for the winged Victory figure in this work
Sculpture: Robert Lowell's poem 'For the Union Dead' calls this work 'a fishbone in the city's throat'
Sculpture: depicted in the first of Charles Ives' Three Pieces in New England
Sculpture: 34 stars line the top; angel holds olive branch and poppies
Sculpture: president rising from/standing before a chair with eagle motif, located in Lincoln Park, subtitled 'The Man'
Sculpture: shrouded woman resting cheek on right hand, inspired by Japanese Buddhist art introduced by John La Farge
Sculpture: honors a woman who committed suicide by ingesting potassium cyanide
Sculpture: equestrian statue in Manhattan's Grand Army Plaza with gilded Victory (modeled on Winged Victory of Samothrace) holding a palm frond
Sculpture: this sculptor's last monument features a replica of a famous headless Greek sculpture in front of a Union general
Sculpture: recurring motif of winged angel holding tablet, wearing belt and crown of passion flowers, originally for tomb of Edwin Morgan
Sculpture: nude weathervane for Madison Square Garden, commissioned by Stanford White; clothed to satisfy Anthony Comstock but clothes blew off
Sculpture: design of torch-bearing nude in contrapposto was overturned, starting feud with Charles Barber
Artist: designed the $20 Double Eagle gold coin for the US Mint (produced 1907-1933); relief had to be lowered
Artist: Liberty strides forward with olive branch on obverse of this gold coin
Artist: founded the Cornish Art Colony in New Hampshire
Artist: born in Dublin; near end of life created monument honoring Charles Stuart Parnell for his birthplace
Artist: created bronze relief medallion portrait of Robert Louis Stevenson in bed writing
Artist: designed four caryatids (Art, Music, Painting, Sculpture) for the Albright-Knox Art Gallery facade in Buffalo
Artist: involved in the City Beautiful movement; proposed McMillan plan for the National Mall with Charles McKim
